tire_description.name: Tire description
tire_description.intro_line_1: The {{ vendor }} {{ title }} is a {{ season }} {{ vehicle_type }} tire available in size {{ tire_size }}.
tire_description.intro_rated: Rated with a {{ load_index }} load index and {{ speed_rating }} speed rating.
tire_description.intro_load_capacity: Supports up to {{ max_load_lbs }} lbs per tire.
tire_description.intro_speed_capacity: Safely rated for speeds up to {{ max_speed_kmh }} km/h.
tire_description.intro_warranty: Backed by a {{ warranty_km }} km treadwear warranty from {{ vendor }} for long-lasting value and peace of mind.
tire_description.intro_3pmsf: Certified with the Three-Peak Mountain Snowflake (3PMSF) symbol, this tire meets Transport Canada's severe snow service standards — an excellent choice for Calgary's harsh winters.
tire_description.intro_ms: Marked with the M+S (Mud and Snow) designation for light winter capability. For Calgary's harshest winter months, we recommend pairing with dedicated winter tires.
tire_description.intro_available: Available at Prince Tires in Calgary, Alberta with professional mounting, balancing, and installation services.
tire_description.local_winter: Calgary experiences average winter temperatures of -7°C with frequent drops below -20°C. Combined with ice, packed snow, and freeze-thaw cycles on roads like Deerfoot Trail and Stoney Trail, a quality winter tire is essential. The {{ title }} is engineered for these conditions, providing confident stopping and traction when temperatures drop.
tire_description.local_all_season: The {{ title }} can be used in a variety of weather conditions, including dry and wet roads from spring through fall. However, all-season tires are not designed for Calgary's winter season and should not be used as a year-round solution in this region. Calgary winters are severe, with temperatures regularly dropping below -20°C, icy roads, and heavy snowfall. Driving on all-season tires during Calgary's winter is dangerous and a serious safety concern. We always recommend switching to dedicated winter tires from November through March — your safety and the safety of others on the road depends on it.
tire_description.local_all_weather: The {{ title }} is designed for year-round use in Canadian climates. However, for the most severe Calgary winter conditions, a dedicated winter tire will provide better ice and deep snow performance.
tire_description.local_all_weather_3pmsf: The {{ title }} is designed for year-round use in Canadian climates like Calgary's. With the Three-Peak Mountain Snowflake certification, this tire meets severe snow performance standards — meaning you can drive confidently through Calgary winters without seasonal tire swaps.
tire_description.local_summer: The {{ title }} is optimized for warm-weather driving performance. This tire is not suitable for Calgary's winter conditions (November through March). Pair with a dedicated winter tire set for year-round driving in Alberta.
tire_description.local_default: Contact Prince Tires Calgary for expert advice on whether the {{ title }} is the right fit for your vehicle and local driving conditions.
tire_description.faq_1_q: Is the {{ title }} a good tire for driving in Calgary?
tire_description.faq_1_a_winter: Yes. The {{ title }} is an excellent choice for Calgary winters. With temperatures regularly dropping below -20°C and roads covered in ice and packed snow, a dedicated winter tire like this provides the stopping power and traction you need on Deerfoot Trail, Stoney Trail, and city streets.
tire_description.faq_1_a_all_season: The {{ title }} is a good choice for Calgary's spring, summer, and fall driving. However, it is not designed for Calgary's severe winter conditions and should not be used as a year-round tire in this region. Using all-season tires during Calgary winters is dangerous — temperatures regularly fall below -20°C with icy roads and heavy snow. We always recommend dedicated winter tires from November through March for your safety.
tire_description.faq_1_a_all_weather: The {{ title }} is suitable for year-round driving in Calgary. While it handles light winter conditions, for the coldest months with heavy snow and ice, a dedicated winter tire may provide better performance.
tire_description.faq_1_a_all_weather_3pmsf: Yes. The {{ title }} carries the Three-Peak Mountain Snowflake (3PMSF) certification, meaning it meets severe snow performance standards. This makes it a strong year-round option for Calgary drivers who prefer not to swap tires seasonally.
tire_description.faq_1_a_summer: The {{ title }} is designed for warm-weather performance only. It should not be used during Calgary's winter months (November through March). Visit Prince Tires to find a matching winter tire set for seasonal use.
tire_description.faq_1_a_default: Contact Prince Tires Calgary for personalized advice on whether this tire suits your vehicle and local driving conditions. Our team can recommend the best option based on your driving habits.
tire_description.faq_2_q: How long does the {{ title }} last?
tire_description.faq_2_a_full: The {{ title }} comes with a {{ warranty_km }} km treadwear warranty and has a UTQG treadwear rating of {{ utqg_treadwear }}. With proper maintenance including regular rotation and alignment, you can expect reliable performance throughout the warranty period. Visit Prince Tires Calgary for free tread depth checks.
tire_description.faq_2_a_warranty: The {{ title }} comes with a {{ warranty_km }} km treadwear warranty from the manufacturer. With proper maintenance including regular rotation and alignment, you can expect reliable performance throughout the warranty period.
tire_description.faq_2_a_utqg: The {{ title }} has a UTQG treadwear rating of {{ utqg_treadwear }}. Higher numbers indicate longer expected tread life. With proper maintenance including regular rotation and alignment, this tire will deliver reliable longevity.
tire_description.faq_3_q: What is the load capacity of the {{ title }}?
tire_description.faq_3_a_full: The {{ title }} has a load index of {{ load_index }}, which means each tire can safely support up to {{ max_load_lbs }} lbs. For a set of four, that is a combined capacity of {{ max_load_lbs }} lbs per tire. Always ensure your tire's load rating meets or exceeds your vehicle manufacturer's specifications.
tire_description.faq_3_a_basic: The {{ title }} has a load index of {{ load_index }}. You can find the specific weight capacity for this load index on the tire sidewall or by contacting Prince Tires Calgary. Always ensure your tire's load rating meets your vehicle manufacturer's specifications.
tire_description.faq_4_q: Does the {{ title }} have the snowflake symbol (3PMSF)?
tire_description.faq_4_a_yes: Yes, the {{ title }} carries the Three-Peak Mountain Snowflake (3PMSF) symbol. This means it has been independently tested and meets the required performance criteria in severe snow conditions as defined by Transport Canada and the Rubber Association of Canada.
tire_description.faq_4_a_no: No, the {{ title }} does not carry the Three-Peak Mountain Snowflake (3PMSF) symbol. If you need a tire certified for severe snow conditions in Calgary, visit Prince Tires and our team will help you find the right winter or all-weather tire.
tire_description.faq_5_q: Does Prince Tires offer installation for this tire?
tire_description.faq_5_a: Yes. Prince Tires Calgary offers professional tire installation including mounting, balancing, valve stems, and disposal of your old tires. We also provide wheel alignment, TPMS service, and seasonal tire storage. Book your installation appointment online or call us directly.
